commit.message:
refactor: identifier les credentials AAP par username et non par name

Le champ username (inputs.username) devient l'identifiant permanent :
  username = SYNCH_TOKENS_GITLAB_<PATH_ENCODÉ>

- token_filters.py : parse_synch_credentials filtre sur inputs.username
  au lieu de name ; decode le path depuis username ; suppression de
  token_name (n'est plus disponible via username)
- 01_fetch_aap_credentials.yml : retire name__startswith de la query AAP
  (inputs.username non filtrable via API) → filtrage client-side
- 02_process_token.yml : PATCH AAP n'ecrase plus username (identifiant
  permanent) → uniquement password mis a jour ; suppression lookup
  ancien token (token_name absent)
- 03_send_report.yml, 04_create_gitlab_issues.yml : retire les champs
  old_token_name/id/expires/old_revoked du rapport (plus disponibles)
- email_dry_run.j2, email_report.j2 : retire les colonnes inutiles
- group_vars/all.yml, renew_gitlab_tokens.yml : commentaires mis a jour

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- app/src/main.py: add gitops=true flag to / response (dummy change to
  trigger CI build and GHCR image push)

commit.message:
Add numbered superscript affiliations for multi-author papers

New rev.yaml syntax:
  authors:
    - name: Author Name
      affiliations: [a, b]
      corresponding: true
      email: email@example.com
  affiliations:
    a: Department, University, Country
    b: School, University, Country

PDF/TEX: generates authblk LaTeX with \author[1,2]{...} and \affil[1]{...}
DOCX: generates markdown with ^superscript^ affiliation numbers
Backward compatible — simple author lists still work as before.

commit.message:
Fix double-zoom in CDN template (same coordinate space bug as offline)

Apply the same fix as the offline template: wrap all SVG children in a
new <g> to preserve the Graphviz <g>'s transform, and compute fitView
entirely in viewBox coordinate space to avoid double-scaling.

Also fix zoomToBBox and the node-click handler to work in viewBox
coordinate space: node rects are converted from screen coordinates using
vbInfo() rather than passing getBBox() values (Graphviz coordinate space)
directly to a function expecting viewBox-space coordinates.

commit.message:
fix: neighborhood listings — use ZIP codes for Trestle query, bounds for post-filter

Trestle IDX Plus does not expose Latitude/Longitude for OData filtering,
causing bounds-only queries to fail with 502. Fix: send ZIP codes as the
OData filter (server push), then post-filter results by lat/lng bounding
box on the server for precise neighborhood boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix product image CORS: use fetch+blob instead of crossOrigin img

Firebase Storage doesn't return CORS headers for crossOrigin image
loads from GitHub Pages. Using fetch→blob→objectURL bypasses this
since fetch doesn't taint the canvas like crossOrigin does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refine: shared intelligence framing + update demo script

The core distinction: separate agents on the same repo isn't a network.
GitHub's bots all post to the same PR thread. None can query the others'
analysis. Federation lets agents access each other's PRIVATE capabilities
(scoring engine, 63K-chunk search index) - not just read public comments.

Slide 4: "His agent queries my scoring engine. My agent queries his
63K-chunk search. Not reading the same PR thread - accessing each
other's intelligence. GitHub's bots can't do that."

Slide 6: "Trucks on the same road aren't a network. Shared routing
data is. Not separate agents. Shared intelligence. That's the multiplier."

DEMO-SCRIPT: Updated timing table (9 slides), added Slide 6 (Bottleneck
Rule) speaking section, renumbered all slides 7-9, updated punchline
to 4 merged PRs, refreshed key phrases and narrative one-liner.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): centre GEX ladder on zero axis + label layout + contracts count

Chart fixes:
- x-axis is now symmetric: min=-maxAbs, max=+maxAbs on every render,
  so the zero line sits exactly in the middle regardless of call/put
  GEX imbalance. Updated on each 60s refresh as maxAbs changes.
- Vertical centre line drawn at x=0 via hLines plugin (rgba white, 13%)
  to visually anchor the calls-right / puts-left split.
- SPOT label: positioned on the RIGHT side of the chart (pill anchored
  at xs.right), consistent with GEXBot Classic reference.
- Level labels (ZERO GEX, CALL WALL, PUT WALL): positioned on the LEFT
  side (pill anchored at xs.left) so they never compete with bars.

Backend + API:
- radar_engine.get_radar() now returns contracts_used (int) = number of
  contracts used for the histogram / walls calculation.
- RadarResponse Pydantic model includes contracts_used: int.
- Mock fallback sets contracts_used = 0.

Frontend:
- Status footer shows "N contracts" below timestamp and countdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(codecraft): redesign with parallel agents, /codecraft command, and governance audit

Inspired by Anthropic's feature-dev plugin. Major changes:

- Add /codecraft command: single entry point orchestrating the full
  7-phase arc with explicit user gates between phases
- Add explorer agent: parallel codebase exploration (trace execution
  paths, map architecture, extract patterns)
- Add architect agent: parallel architecture proposals (minimal,
  clean, pragmatic approaches for user selection)
- Add auditor agent + /audit skill: 12-rule Python governance audit
  with 3 parallel agent groups (architecture, type safety, tooling)
- Update reviewer: >=80% confidence threshold, scored findings
- Update /understand: launches 2-3 parallel explorer agents
- Update /design: launches 2-3 parallel architect agents
- Update /verify: now 4-pass gate — correctness, compliance, review,
  and mandatory governance audit scoped to changed files
- Remove system/ directory (8 files): consolidated ENTRYPOINT.md,
  SKILL.md, and 6 workflow files into command and updated skills

Net: 23 files → 20 files, ~950 lines of duplication removed.

commit.message:
fix: replace @dnd-kit with HTML5 native drag-and-drop

@dnd-kit was crashing the app (black screen) on every drag attempt,
likely due to race conditions between PointerSensor events and React's
render cycle in production builds.

Replaced with browser-native HTML5 DnD:
- Each panel is draggable via the grip handle
- Dropping on a panel inserts before it; dropping on an empty column area appends
- No external library — draggable attr + onDragStart/onDragOver/onDrop events
- Visual feedback: dragged panel goes to 0.4 opacity, drop target gets gold outline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): set LD_LIBRARY_PATH for FFI shared library at runtime (#44)

* fix(ci): set LD_LIBRARY_PATH for FFI shared library at runtime

The CGo LDFLAGS `-L` flag tells the compile-time linker where to find
libexperimentation_ffi.so, but on Linux the runtime dynamic linker
also needs LD_LIBRARY_PATH to locate the .so when executing test
binaries. Without this, tests fail with:

  error while loading shared libraries: libexperimentation_ffi.so:
  cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

Set LD_LIBRARY_PATH to target/debug/ for both unit and integration
test steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): use valid base64 Kafka CLUSTER_ID for KRaft mode

The previous CLUSTER_ID ("ci-test-cluster-id-00001") was 25 characters
with hyphens, which is not valid base64. KRaft mode requires a
22-character base64-encoded UUID for kafka-storage format. This caused
the Kafka container to exit(1) on startup, failing integration tests.

Also adds container log capture on docker compose failure for easier
debugging.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): fix Kafka tmpfs permissions and Postgres healthcheck DB

Kafka: cp-kafka runs as appuser (uid 1000) but the tmpfs mount at
/var/lib/kafka/data is owned by root, causing the writable check to
fail. Run as root (user: "0:0") since this is an ephemeral CI container.

Postgres: pg_isready without -d defaults to connecting to a database
named after the user ("test"), but the actual DB is "experimentation_test".
Add explicit -d flag to the healthcheck.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): align Postgres credentials with service defaults and mount schema

All Go services default to postgres://experimentation:localdev@localhost:5432/experimentation.
Updated docker-compose.test.yml to match so integration tests work
without needing DATABASE_URL overrides.

Also mount sql/migrations/ into docker-entrypoint-initdb.d so the
schema is applied automatically on container startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): apply SQL migrations explicitly with two-pass FK resolution

The docker-entrypoint-initdb.d approach fails because 001_schema.sql
creates experiments before its FK targets (layers, targeting_rules,
surrogate_models). Postgres rejects REFERENCES to non-existent tables.

Instead, apply migrations via psql after containers are healthy. Two
passes handle the ordering: pass 1 creates independent tables, pass 2
creates FK-dependent tables that failed in pass 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): fix management service compilation errors blocking CI

- Rename exp.Guardrails → exp.GuardrailConfigs to match proto field name
- Add missing "fmt" import in metric_integration_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): seed reference data for integration tests

Integration tests expect layers, metrics, and targeting rules to exist
in the database. Run seed_dev.sql after schema migrations to provide
this reference data, fixing "metric does not exist" and FK constraint
violations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): apply schema and seed data inside postgres container

The external psql commands were silently failing (psql not reliably
available on the CI runner). Move schema migrations and seed data
application into docker-entrypoint-initdb.d so they run inside the
postgres container during startup, before the healthcheck passes.

- Add scripts/init-test-db.sh: two-pass migration + seed execution
- Mount SQL files and init script into postgres via docker-compose
- Remove external psql commands from ci.yml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve remaining integration test failures

- Use seeded metric ID "watch_time_minutes" in stream tests (was "metric-1")
- Add timeout-guarded pool.Close() in guardrail tests to prevent 10m hang
- Add -timeout 2m to integration test command to cap future hangs
- Show stderr on pass 2 of init-test-db.sh for better CI debugging

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): correct audit_trail column names in seed data

The seed_dev.sql used `actor` and `details` but the schema has
`actor_email` and `details_json`. Since the seed runs inside a
BEGIN/COMMIT transaction, this error rolled back ALL seed data
(layers, metrics, targeting rules), leaving the DB empty and
causing FK violations in every integration test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(gitops): ArgoCD GitOps CD pipeline

* feat(ci): add GitHub Actions build pipeline, Helm chart, and GitOps plan

- .github/workflows/build-release.yml
    • Triggers only on app/** changes; helm/** changes never fire a build
    • Builds Docker image tagged with full 40-char git SHA (ghcr.io)
    • Patches helm/app/values.yaml with the new tag via yq
    • Commits back with [skip ci] + path filter = two-layer loop prevention
    • Matrix-ready for multi-service expansion
    • Docker layer caching, provenance attestation, SBOM enabled

- helm/app/
    • Full Helm chart: Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, HPA, helpers
    • Deployment pinned to exact SHA tag; rolling-update, anti-affinity
    • values.yaml: image.tag field clearly marked as CI-managed

- app/
    • Minimal FastAPI placeholder (Python 3.12 multi-stage Dockerfile)
    • Runs as non-root UID 1000, readOnlyRootFilesystem

- docs/gitops-argocd-plan.md
    • Full architecture diagram and ArgoCD Application YAML
    • Trigger matrix, loop-prevention rationale, rollback procedure
    • Multi-service extension guide, secrets setup, observability hooks

- docs/claude-agent-prompt.md
    • Detailed work order for a Claude Code agent to implement ArgoCD
      Application manifests, env overlays, deployment events, Dependabot

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Kech4jxkWCxcg5343j2j2C

* chore: add terminal PR creation script and manual steps

docs/open-pr.sh covers three paths:
  1. gh CLI  (bash docs/open-pr.sh --gh)
  2. curl + GITHUB_TOKEN REST API
  3. Fallback URL printed if no auth available

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Kech4jxkWCxcg5343j2j2C

* feat(gitops): implement ArgoCD GitOps CD pipeline

Task 1 — ArgoCD manifests:
- argocd/application-app.yaml: production Application with automated sync,
  prune/selfHeal, CreateNamespace, 5-retry exponential backoff
- argocd/application-app-staging.yaml: staging variant with valueFiles overlay
- argocd/project.yaml: AppProject 'platform' scoped to this repo
- argocd/notifications-cm.yaml: Slack alerts for sync-failed, sync-succeeded,
  health-degraded using $slack-token secret reference

Task 2 — Image pull secret automation:
- scripts/create-pull-secret.sh: idempotent GHCR pull secret creator,
  accepts --namespace/--username/--token/--registry, passes shellcheck

Task 3 — Helm environment overlays:
- helm/app/values-staging.yaml: replicas=1, reduced resources, tag=latest
- helm/app/values-production.yaml: replicas=3, HPA enabled, stricter resources
- argocd/application-app-staging.yaml references both values.yaml files

Task 4 — GitHub Deployment event reporting:
- build-release.yml: new notify-deployment job after update-helm succeeds
- Posts Deployment event (POST /deployments) then updates status to success
- Reads SERVICE_URL from repo variable; skips environment_url if unset
- Adds deployments: write permission

Task 5 — Dependabot:
- .github/dependabot.yml: weekly updates for Actions, Docker (app/),
  and Python packages (app/src/requirements.txt)

Task 6 — Documentation:
- README.md: CI/CD & GitOps section with ASCII flow diagram, add-service
  guide, rollback procedures, and required secrets/variables table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rebrand: AmbientCore → AmbientOS, ConversionCore → AmbientScore

Dual product rebrand across 90 files:

AmbientCore → AmbientOS:
- Rename ambientcore/ → ambientos/, images/AmbientCore/ → images/AmbientOS/
- Update all text, CSS classes (.ambientcore-* → .ambientos-*), URLs
- 301 redirect /ambientcore/* → /ambientos/

ConversionCore → AmbientScore:
- Rename conversioncore/ → ambientscore/, api/_lib/conversionCore/ → ambientScore/
- Rename api/cc-* routes → api/as-*
- CSS prefix --cc-/`.cc-` → --as-/.as- (AmbientScore only, not Command Center)
- "Conversion Health Score" → "AmbientScore" (score IS the brand)
- 301 redirect /conversioncore/* → /ambientscore/

Backward compat preserved:
- record.conversioncore data property (existing blobs)
- cc_credits_ storage key (customer credits)
- conversioncore_strategy in VALID_TYPES (alongside ambientscore_strategy)
- Env var fallbacks: AS_SITE_URL||CC_SITE_URL, AMBIENTOS_INTERNAL_KEY||AMBIENTCORE_INTERNAL_KEY

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
